bead.title: review: enforce budget/refusal contracts for review turns
bead.description:
PROBLEM
This bead was scoped around preferred_reviewer, downgrade routing, and Story 10 ladder fallback, but ADR-024 removed that configuration-heavy behavior from the default path. The still-valid gap is narrower: review turns, including adversarial pre-close review and server review sessions, need bounded budget/refusal behavior and structured errors that do not silently skip review or accidentally close work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22 stores review session state but does not define a per-turn refusal/error contract for budget or prompt overflow.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474 builds reviewer dispatch runtime but does not carry review_group_id/reviewer_index budget/refusal context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:780 classifies parse/provider-empty errors in the reviewer path, but server review-session refusal codes remain separate and incomplete.
- The previous bead text required preferred_reviewer and downgrade behavior that is no longer part of the default adversarial review policy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove preferred_reviewer/downgrade routing from this bead's implementation scope.
- Enforce max_billable_usd for server review sessions and adversarial review turns with structured refusal body when a new turn would exceed the cap.
- Return stable refusal codes PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, and REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE where those conditions are truly terminal for a review turn.
- For the pre-close gate, budget/refusal outcomes must feed the review_error retry/exhaustion path rather than being treated as successful review or silently skipped.
- Emit review-pairing-degraded only as telemetry when pairing is weaker than intended; do not use it to downgrade or mutate route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the pre-close two-reviewer coordinator; tracked by ddx-c851c3dd.
- GraphQL display of refusal bodies.
- Preferred reviewer policy or configurable reviewer downgrade ladder.
bead.acceptance:
1. max_billable_usd is enforced before each server review turn and before each adversarial reviewer invocation; exceeding it returns COST_CAP_EXCEEDED with structured JSON body and does not close a bead.
2. PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, and REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE return stable structured refusal bodies with code, message, retryable, and operator_action fields.
3. Pre-close review budget/refusal outcomes are classified as review_error and flow through the review_max_retries / review-manual-required path from ADR-024.
4. review-pairing-degraded remains telemetry only; no preferred_reviewer or downgrade ladder behavior is implemented by this bead.
5. TestReview_CostCap_ReturnsCostCapExceeded verifies session cap enforcement.
6. TestPreCloseReview_CostCapFeedsReviewError verifies adversarial review cap exhaustion prevents close and records review_error.
7. TestReview_RefusalCodes_HaveStructuredBody verifies all three refusal codes return JSON bodies.
8. TestReviewPairingDegraded_IsTelemetryOnly verifies degraded pairing does not mutate harness/model/profile pins or downgrade route.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: This bead is over-scoped for the current server worktree. The server review code only has session persistence and prompt assembly in cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-239, while the actual reviewer routing, downgrade telemetry, and prompt-refusal handling live in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474-780. Cost-cap enforcement for the worker loop already exists in cli/internal/server/workers.go:790-855, but there is no server-side turn dispatcher to hook the requested "before each turn" max_billable_usd check into. Satisfying all acceptance criteria would require crossing into the non-scope dispatcher/routing path and depending on the ddx-0e5c3005 / ddx-f7c3d512 follow-on work, so I am stopping here rather than partially implementing an incomplete contract.

no_changes
